short_description: Integrated scheme for school education from pre-school to class 12 with focus on quality and equity.
detailed_description: Samagra Shiksha is an overarching programme for the school education sector extending from pre-school to class 12. The scheme subsumes the three erstwhile Centrally Sponsored Schemes of Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA), Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan (RMSA) and Teacher Education (TE). It aims to ensure inclusive and equitable quality education from pre-primary to senior secondary stage in accordance with the Sustainable Development Goal for Education.
key_benefits: Support for children with special needs
other_conditions: Student must be enrolled in government or government-aided school
Focus on disadvantaged groups and areas

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Proof of enrollment in government school

application_steps_offline: Enroll in government or government-aided school
School will automatically provide benefits under Samagra Shiksha
Textbooks and uniforms distributed by school
Mid-day meals provided during school hours
No separate application required for basic benefits

faq.question: Do I need to apply separately for textbooks and uniforms?
faq.answer: No, once enrolled in a government school, these benefits are provided automatically under Samagra Shiksha.

faq.question: Is mid-day meal provided to all students?
faq.answer: Yes, mid-day meals are provided to all students from class 1 to 8 in government and government-aided schools.

faq.question: Are private school students eligible?
faq.answer: No, Samagra Shiksha benefits are primarily for students in government and government-aided schools.

tips_for_successful_application: Enroll in government or government-aided school
